heading: Sixto Ramos geometry packet separates source-side machine witnesses from later QA reinterpretation attempts
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, arto-heino, sixto-ramos, external-prior-art, geometry-packet]
body: Built `sixto_ramos_geometry_packet.json` and `build_sixto_ramos_geometry_packet.py` as the first source-graded packet under the Arto contra-cyclic lane. Stable source-side items: (1) the Arto article explicitly says the Sixto Ramos machine relates to Archimedean twin-circle (Fibonacci/golden) geometry, toroidal geometry, and part of the conchoid of de Sluze family; (2) the article exposes a concrete Sixto asset set `SixtoRam3b`, `Sioxto3002B`, `SixtWave2`, `Sioxto4001D`, and `CCH-Archm1b`; and (3) it explicitly says a geometric diagram includes the Archimedean twin circles and the basic geometry of the Ramos machine. The later local Archimedean-radius note and the 2025 QA mapping note are preserved only as reinterpretation candidates, not promoted into the source layer. Honest verdict: the packet is ready, but the geometry itself is still raster-level; the next substantive step is to extract the Sixto diagram topology into a machine-readable witness before any QA/RT law is promoted.
